title: Agent Teams
description: The lead spawns teammate loops, communicates through inboxes, and injects teammate results back into history.

steps.content: This backend refactor is too broad for one agent. Create a teammate for the database work.
steps.annotation: The user asks for work that exceeds a single agent's attention budget.

steps.content: I'll spawn a backend teammate and give her the schema task.
steps.annotation: The lead decides to delegate to a persistent teammate rather than a one-shot subagent.

steps.content: {"name":"alice","role":"backend developer","prompt":"Create schema.sql with a users table."}
steps.annotation: The new tool starts a teammate loop with its own messages and prompt.
The teammate is now active and has an inbox on the message bus.

steps.content: alice reads .mailboxes/alice.jsonl, writes schema.sql, then sends a result to lead.
steps.annotation: Teammates work independently and communicate through file-style inboxes.

steps.content: lead inbox consumed -> From alice: schema.sql created with users table.
steps.annotation: Inbox messages are injected into the lead's history so the model can react.

steps.content: Alice finished the schema work. I'll use her result in the next step of the refactor.
steps.annotation: The lead incorporates teammate output as normal conversation context.
